content.text:
{
  "dimension": "perceptual-accuracy",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach repeatedly names what he's seeing in real time and grounds his questions in those reads. Turn 2 ('just like right now') reads the live anxiety in the room. Turn 14-16 names a specific somatic movement ('you started sinking down into yourself') and distinguishes the said from the shown ('you answered, but you didn't keep exploring the I don't know'). Turn 18 names the emotion that arrived ('that was grief') rather than asking about it — a hallmark level-3 read. Turn 22 ('Yeah, yeah, I see it. Don't try to explain it') reads a shift before the client can verbalize it. Reads consistently precede questions and land at the moment they change the conversation.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 14, 16, 18, 22],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Immediate read of the live anxiety — names that the topic is happening right now."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the present-moment dynamic: 'you're being seen. How is that working for you?'"},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Notices a somatic 'going down' movement and asks the client to stay with it rather than complete the verbal answer."},
    {"turn": 16, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Specific somatic description ('sinking down into yourself') and distinguishes shown from said."},
    {"turn": 18, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the grief that arrived rather than asking what the client felt — read precedes question."},
    {"turn": 22, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Reads the shift before client can articulate it ('I see it. Don't try to explain it')."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["rehearsing-how-to-say-it", "looking-for-whats-wrong", "how-is-my-anxiety-right-now", "how-is-that-not-here-right-now"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["defensiveness-as-identity-signal", "fear-triangle"],
  "notes": "Clean example of reads driving the inquiry throughout."
}
